No abnormality. Excludes structural cause for symptoms.

note.text: PEG tube placed without complication. Tolerated procedure well.
BiPAP initiated for nocturnal hypoventilation. IPAP 14, EPAP 6.

resource.title: Multidisciplinary ALS care plan
resource.description: Coordinated care across neurology, pulmonology, nutrition, PT/OT/SLP, social work, and palliative care. Visits every 3 months.

resource.title: Palliative care consult
resource.description: Palliative care team engaged for symptom management, advance care planning, and family support.

resource.title: Communication strategies
resource.description: Speech-language pathology to introduce alternative augmentative communication device. Voice banking initiated.

resource.conclusion: Electromyography demonstrates widespread fibrillation potentials and positive sharp waves in cervical, thoracic, and lumbar paraspinal muscles. Motor unit potentials show large amplitude, long duration, and reduced recruitment patterns. Nerve conduction studies reveal preserved sensory responses with reduced motor amplitudes. Findings consistent with motor neuron disease.

No acute intracranial abnormality. Cortical and subcortical structures within normal limits. No evidence of structural lesion accounting for the patient's symptoms. Excludes mimics of motor neuron disease.
FVC 62% predicted (down from 78% three months ago). MIP -45 cm H2O. MEP +60 cm H2O. Findings consistent with progressive respiratory muscle weakness. NIV indicated.

description.text: Maintain functional independence as long as possible through OT, adaptive equipment, and home modifications.

target.detailString: Maintain functional independence as long as possible through OT, adaptive equipment, and home modifications.

description.text: Manage symptoms (spasticity, sialorrhea, dysphagia, fatigue) to optimize quality of life.

target.detailString: Manage symptoms (spasticity, sialorrhea, dysphagia, fatigue) to optimize quality of life.

description.text: Complete advance care planning including MOLST and discussion of long-term ventilation preferences.
